question_text: Energy in food can be measured in both kilojoules (kJ) and kilocalories (kcal). Which of the following is true about these units?

options.label: B
options.text: 1 kJ and 1 kcal are the same size

options.label: D
options.text: kcal is only used for measuring heat, not food energy

explanation: 1 kilocalorie equals approximately 4.2 kilojoules, so a kilocalorie is a larger unit. Both are used to measure food energy, but kilojoules are now more commonly used in the UK.
